channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: California man allegedly murdered roommate, buried body in backyard: police
item.description: A California man was arrested after allegedly killing his roommate and burying the body in his backyard following an altercation with the victim.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Georgia parents charged with child cruelty and false imprisonment of 4 teenagers
item.description: Two Georgia parents are facing charges over accusations of child cruelty and false imprisonment of their four teenagers who were living under conditions described as inhumane.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lynx force Game 5 of WNBA Finals on Carleton's free throws to beat Liberty 82-80
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — With their season on the line, the Minnesota Lynx did just enough to stave off elimination and force a decisive Game 5 of the WNBA Finals. Bridget Carleton made two free throws with 2 seconds left, and the Lynx beat the New York Liberty 82-80 on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Black male teachers are a rarity in preschools. This pioneering program wants to change that
item.description: A fellowship program is training young Black men to become literacy aides in preschool classrooms across the country.
item.dc:creator: Moriah Balingit, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Buy a tiny house at Walmart? You can for under $16K
item.description: While there are dozens of things to consider before purchasing a tiny home, it's never been easier to find and buy one.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, WGN 9

item.title: Jury paves the way for 2 Live Crew to retake control of records that changed hip-hop
item.description: Miami's 2 Live Crew helped redraw the legal landscape around what hip-hop could be, pushing the boundaries of free speech and taste with their provocative and sexually explicit recordings that led to landmark court decisions protecting the rights of artists.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Liam Payne's father arrives in Buenos Aires days after the pop star's fatal hotel plunge
item.description: The father of former One Direction star Liam Payne arrived in Buenos Aires on Friday to arrange for the return of his son's body to England two days after the famous boy band singer fell to his death from a hotel balcony.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Mexican cartel leader 'El Mayo' Zambada makes court appearance in his US drug trafficking case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Ismael “El Mayo” Zambada, a longtime leader of Mexico’s Sinaloa cartel who is facing drug trafficking charges in a U.S. court, appeared for the first time Friday before the judge who is presiding over his case.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 20,000 people are missing in Baja California, group says
item.description: "We don't believe every missing person is a victim of something malicious or a crime, maybe they fell to addiction or have no way of communicating with anyone from the streets, that's why we have to go looking.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Deadly Homecoming shooting suspect charged with federal gun crime
item.description: Marquez Davis, 24, has now been charged relating to the shooting that killed one person on Tennessee State's Homecoming weekend on Oct. 12.
item.dc:creator: Erin McCullough, NewsNation

item.title: Trump tells child there will be no cows under Harris
item.description: Former President Donald Trump joined Fox and Friends on Friday for an in-person interview where he told a 6-year-old child that the U.S. “won’t have any cows” if Vice President Kamala Harris is elected.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, ABC 4

item.title: Trump compares imprisoned Jan. 6 rioters to Japanese Americans during WWII
item.description: Nearly 900 people have pleaded guilty to a variety of federal charges in relation to the events of Jan. 6.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 6 Florida counties saw 'unusual increase' in flesh-eating bacteria after hurricanes: health officials
item.description: Florida health officials noted that six Tampa Bay area counties saw an "unusual increase" in cases of Vibrio vulnificus this year, attributed to floodwaters from back-to-back hurricanes.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Oregon elections office shuts down phone lines amid false info surrounding Trump, voters' pamphlet
item.description: The Oregon Elections Division is shutting down its phone lines after the system was "overwhelmed" by out-of-state callers responding to false information about why Donald Trump is not in the Oregon Voters' Pamphlet.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Kustura, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge rules Delphi composite sketches aren't admissible
item.description: UPDATE: During Friday's proceedings, Judge Fran Gull ruled that the sketches aren't admissible in court. The state filed a motion this week seeking to prohibit the use of the two widely circulated sketches from the early years of the investigation.
item.dc:creator: Matt Adams, NewsNation

item.title: Abortion is on the ballot in 10 states. Here's where the measures stand
item.description: Voters in 10 states will decide this November whether to adopt constitutional amendments that could shape the future of abortion access in their states.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, Fox 5

item.title: Acting CBP commissioner says fentanyl seizures are up and illegal migration is down
item.description: “In the last two fiscal year, CBP has seized more than 50,000 pounds of fentanyl, that’s more than enough to produce more than two million lethal doses of fentanyl,” said Miller.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, Fox 5

item.title: Menendez brothers' uncle requests DA meeting; says boys' motive was greed
item.description: Anderson believes Erik and Lyle Menendez weren't molested by their father Jose and that "justice was served.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Whooping cough is at a decade-high level in US
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — Whooping cough is at its highest level in a decade for this time of year, U.S. health officials reported Thursday. There have been 18,506 cases of whooping cough reported so far, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention said.
item.dc:creator: DEVI SHASTRI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The Biden administration has canceled student loans for more than 1 million in public service jobs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A student loan cancellation program for public service workers has granted relief to more than 1 million Americans — up from just 7,000 who were approved before it was updated by the Biden administration two years ago.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Liam Payne's family releases statement as more details, autopsy results emerge in singer's death
item.description: Payne, 31, died after a fall from the third story of the Casa Sur hotel in Buenos Aires on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: NOAA releases 2024-25 winter weather outlook: Here's what to expect
item.description: Depending on where you live in the U.S., this winter could bring a mix of snow and rain or unseasonably warm weather, according to climate experts.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, ABC 4

item.title: 7-year-old crashes one school bus into another during apparent training in Missouri
item.description: She won't be able to drive for another nine years but parents of an Independence second grader say she's already had her first crash.
item.dc:creator: Dave D'Marko, ABC 4

item.title: 3 states renew efforts to limit abortion pill access
item.description: The lawsuit was filed in a Texas federal court Friday, roughly four months after the Supreme Court decided against undoing changes to the FDA’s regulations on the abortion pill that expanded access — such as allowing it to be mailed without an in-person doctor’s visit.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, KRON4

item.title: Fugitive ex-Olympic snowboarder among 16 charged in connection to transnational drug trafficking, murder ring
item.description: An “Olympic athlete turned drug lord” and 15 others have been charged in a superseding indictment for allegedly running a transnational drug trafficking operation that routinely shipped large amounts of cocaine from Colombia to Canada via Southern California while orchestrating four murders “in furt
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: Homeland Security grants temporary status to Lebanese already in the United States
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration on Thursday announced temporary legal status for Lebanese citizens already in the United States, likely pleasing some Arab voters, including in the battleground state of Michigan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New renderings show how A's ballpark would transform Las Vegas Strip
item.description: Extensive details have been revealed about the proposed $1.5 billion "multi-purpose, enclosed ballpark" on the Las Vegas Strip that could become home to the Las Vegas Athletics MLB franchise in time for the 2028 baseball season.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, Fox 5

item.title: Mitzi Gaynor, ‘South Pacific’ star, dies at 93
item.description: Mitzi Gaynor, the effervescent dancer and actor who starred as Nellie Forbush in the 1958 film "South Pacific" and appeared in other musicals with Bing Crosby, Frank Sinatra and Gene Kelly, has died. She was 93.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: La Nina could soon arrive. Here's what that means for winter weather
item.description: The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration’s Climate Prediction Center says there is a 60% chance that a weak La Nina event will develop this autumn and could last until March.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA O'MALLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Israel's foreign minister confirms that Hamas' top leader Yahya Sinwar was killed in Gaza
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel’s foreign minister has confirmed that Israeli troops in Gaza have killed Hamas’ top leader Yahya Sinwar, a chief architect of last year's attack on Israel that sparked the war.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, SAMY MAGDY and JACK JEFFERY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Universal Orlando announces Epic Universe opening date
item.description: The theme park will feature more than 50 attractions, as well as entertainment, dining and shopping options across five “expansive worlds.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, WGN 9

item.title: Instagram rolls out new features to prevent sextortion scams targeting teens
item.description: Instagram has introduced a series of new safety features to prevent sextortion schemes, marking the platform's latest effort to protect users, especially teenagers, from online harm.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, WGN 9

item.title: Vance says 'no' when asked if Trump lost in 2020
item.description: The answer was the most specific Vance has been on the subject, and a departure from the series of evasions he has offered about the election Trump continues to say without evidence was decided fraudulently.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Listeria recall grows to 12 million pounds of meat and poultry, some of it sent to US schools
item.description: A nationwide recall of meat and poultry products potentially contaminated with listeria has expanded to nearly 12 million pounds and now includes ready-to-eat meals sent to U.S. schools, restaurants and major retailers, federal officials said.
item.dc:creator: JONEL ALECCIA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The son of a South Carolina inmate urges the governor to save his father from execution
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — Richard Moore never meant to kill anyone the night he robbed a South Carolina convenience store and the Black man was convicted by a jury with no African Americans, his son and lawyers say as they fight to save the inmate from execution next month.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Long lost sisters meet for the first time in person
item.description: Two long-lost sisters who met for the first time in person say their love will continue even after this life.
item.dc:creator: Alina Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Convicted killer Susan Smith charged after speaking with filmmaker
item.description: GREENWOOD, S.C. (WSPA) - Convicted killer Susan Smith has been convicted of a disciplinary charge weeks before her parole hearing. Smith, of Union County, was sentenced to life in prison for the murder of her children in Oct. 1994. The SC Department of Corrections confirmed with 7NEWS that on Aug.
item.dc:creator: Nikolette Miller, WGN 9
